rationale: Both candidates independently spot the same design smell (hardcoded default_components duplicating each pipeline's _dit_modules), but neither catches the ground-truth reviewer's actual specific point — driving the mapping from _packed_modules_mapping/stacked_params_mapping to auto-resolve fused-projection naming conflicts — so recall is capped for both. X's extra finding (text-encoder LoRA is still a silent no-op, the same failure mode the PR fixes for unet) is a genuinely valuable, grounded catch, and its review reads as a clean, human-consumable set of actionable comments. Y's findings are similarly grounded but buried under a long raw 'sweep' trace, and its added nit (undocumented _lora_components attribute) is weaker/lower-value than X's, hurting actionability slightly.
